title: Coleman Tent Light
description:
The Coleman Tent Light is a great addition to any camping trip. The strong, built-in magnet allows for easy attachment without damaging the tent fabric. The light operates on four AA batteries (sold separately). The powerful xenon bulb is replaceable. A great addition to any camping trip, the Coleman tent light will keep your tent illuminated during the evening hours. The light attaches to your tent poles easily and securely via the built-in magnet, so you needn't worry about damaging your tent fabric. The light's powerful, replaceable Krypton bulb, meanwhile, offers plenty of light for reading, playing games, or chatting. Best of all, the light--which operates on four AA batteries (sold separately)--won't get in your way while you sleep or hang out, unlike standard lanterns. Made in China, the light includes an easy on/off switch. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: Use a lot of energy

title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
reviewText: Well made, nice design, functional and sharp looking.

title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText:
This is actually the second Coleman Big Game sleeping bag I bought.  This one was purchased for my wife.  She wanted it after I bragged about mine for so long when we camp.  Mine is a year old and I simply love it.

I love the room this bag gives me.  Normal size bags can be difficult to stretch out or turn over in the night.  A mummy bag.... forget it.  I feel tied up and only want to break free.  I am a mover when i sleep and do not stay in one position.  This bag allows me to not only move and stretch but to also let me flex my knee up and well, sleep how I want to as if I am in bed.

Now, the claim for -5 degrees, I am sorry but I cannot see that.  I do get cold easy and am one that loves my Big Buddy heater in the tent with me if it get below 50 degrees.  I have slept without the heater and this Coleman Big Game sleeping bag in 40 degrees and I was cold.  Now, it could be I am 5'7 and about 170 lbs so there is a lot of extra room in this sleeping bag but I do try to keep the top closed around me and squish in the sides when it is cool out, but I still am a bit cold.  I can see the rating of 25 to 30 degrees maybe but again, it could be just me.  But, -5 degrees I think is a bit off...

My wife said she loves this sleeping bag.  She is 5 ft and about smaller framed, but says she sleeps warm and sleeps with it unzipped on the 40 degree nights.  She likes the ability to stretch and move in the bag too.

title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: works like a champ!!

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: I ordered the proper case per model number but it fit so tight I tore the handles off putting the stove in it. wish I didn't waste the money.

title: Coleman 120V Electric Quick Pump, Colors May Vary
description:
Quick Pump, 120 Volt Electric Perhaps no single factor affects your enjoyment of a camping trip as much as a good night's sleep, and it starts with a quality air mattress and fast, convenient pump. The 120-volt Electric Quick Pump, from leading outdoor outfitter Coleman, takes care of half the equation. Coleman's highest output pump, it's designed for use with the company's popular Double Lock Valve, Boston Valve, and Pinch Valve systems, and includes adapters for each type of valve. The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the 120-volt Electric Quick Pump includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
reviewText: I purchased this product solely to use as a pool toy inflator as advertised on the Coleman Web Site which says "Quickly inflate or deflate virtually any airbed or inflatable".  I realize this is meant primarily to be used for inflatable beds, but it does come with an adapter (the pointy one with the loop on the end) that is supposed to be used for other inflatables.  I have an attachment that attaches to our shop vac that is supposed to be used for inflating pool toys, but it is bulky, and difficult to use.  Unfortunately, the adapter on this Coleman Pump isn't any better and does not easily fit into any pool toys that I have, and I have many different sizes and shapes.  It barely fit into the largest ones that I had, and when it did, it didn't do a very good job of inflating the pool toy at all.  It was slow, weak, and extremely loud.  The nozzle and loop is supposed to be used to open the stop valve in the pool toy, but the nozzle was so big, it wouldn't get in there far enough to make an airway.  Unfortunately, they don't offer any smaller adapters.  Overall, this product might be OK at inflating a bed, but as for other inflatables that they advertise about, this isn't the product to do it.  Very Disappointing.

title: Coleman C002 Soft 45 Can Eva Molded Cooler
description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't with the Coleman 45 Can Collapsible Sport Cooler. It's big enough to hold a full day's worth of food and drinks, but still collapses down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. The zippered closure with an integrated bottle opener ensures you're always ready to enjoy a cold drink. For dry goods, the front mesh pocket lets you carry more items while saving room in the main compartment. After you've packed this cooler full, the adjustable shoulder strap and two side handles make it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
reviewText: I bought this for vacations because I thought the idea of such a nice size cooler being collapsible was great. I also loved that it had a hard top with drink holders because I thought theyd be perfect for the beach. Sad truth is the look of this cooler is the only thing it has going for it. I just now had an occasion to use it for the first time and before I drove 14 hours with it in my new SUV, I filled it to test for leaks at home with a mix of ice and water. I left it in my kitchen floor and went shopping. Came back two hours later to a huge puddle surrounding the cooler. I picked it up to take it to empty it and water literally poured out from all sides about 2 up from the bottom of the cooler. I guess it would be okay if you were using ice packs. But if you are using actual ice that melts, this thing is absolute crap. Ill admit that Ive had trouble finding a soft cooler that doesnt leak, but Ive never had one that leaked like this one. I expected so much better from a Coleman product.

title: Coleman Fold and Go Camp Pillow (Small, Colors May Vary)
reviewText: Good for travel and fots in any bag.

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: If you leave the plastic liner in it, it can hold 7 or 8 twist top drinks but there is no room for an ice pack - you would need to use real ice.

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText: This thing is rad. Haven't received that many compliments in a long time. Everyone on our camping trip drooled over this lol.

title: Coleman Rugged Battery Powered Lantern (Family Size)
description:
Lantern, 8D Double U-Tube Rugged Light up your campsite with instant, long-lasting illumination with the Coleman rugged battery-powered lantern. The family-size lantern boasts a 13-watt fluorescent twin U-tube bulb that delivers a bright white light, along with a heavy-duty thermoplastic and rubber housing that holds up to heavy use. More significantly, the lantern will keep your tent well lit for hours, with a runtime of up to 28 hours on the low setting and 18 hours on the high setting (the lantern runs on eight D batteries, sold separately). And should the bulb eventually burn out, you can replace it in seconds thanks to the lantern's quick-release system. Other details include a comfortable handle grip, an easy three-position switch (off, low, and high), a weather- and shatter-resistant exterior, and a nonslip rubberized grip. The lantern--which runs cool thanks to the cool-touch bulb design--measures 9 inches in diameter and 15.25 inches tall and weighs 3.26 pounds without the batteries. About Coleman

reviewText: This chair is okay. I did not enjoy this that much. It seemed very hard for me to get the position of the chair to stay.

description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.

title: Coleman Tent Fan
description: Conquer the outdoors in comfort with help from a Coleman Tent Fan. This portable cooling tool gets the air circulating anywhere in any tent. Just set the magnetic bottom on the ceiling or on the wall where you want it and then attach the steel plate from the outside. The durable, lightweight fan will stay where you want it without slipping or damaging your tent's fabric. The fan is specially-designed not to move when you push the button to turn it on and off, or even when you change the battery. Additional hardware is included to mount it to a building's wall where you need extra ventilation, like a basement or attic.
reviewText: I have a very small trail tent. The fan was only three feet above my face and I could barely feel the breeze of the fan.  It did help with air circulation slightly but not enough for the trouble or money. It broke after maybe half a dozen camping trips or so.

title: Coleman Military Style Canteen
description: Keep your drink close and as cold or hot as you want it in a Coleman Military Style Canteen. This polyethylene canteen leaves no metallic taste. The bottle snaps snuggly into the insulated, cotton cover that clips onto the adjustable belt. A strap even connects the lid to the canteen to prevent you from losing the cap.
reviewText:
Makes an excellent portable johnny cup when you got the urge to go.  Make sure you label it so no one takes a swig out of it.
This unit is a bit smaller than a regular GI Military issued canteen.  Great for a young person to use.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText: this tent, though it's very spacious sucks. it was leaking on the first use. I woke up one morning flitting in a pool of water.

title: Coleman 3-Piece Stainless Steel Nesting Utensil Set
description: Keep your utensils as close as your pocket with the compact Coleman 3-Piece Stainless Steel Nesting Utensil Set. Use the lightweight fork, spoon and knife just like you would at home. When you're done, the dishwasher-safe fork and knife easily slide quickly into the cradle on the spoon handle and fit inside a protective pouch for hands-free carrying. It's perfect for camping, hiking and backpacking.
reviewText: I remember when my brothers were Boy Scouts and they got those cool silverware sets in a plastic envelope.  I cannot imagine where five Boy Scouts lost their flatware, but every one of them complained to me about their losses.  I listened and then I went looking.  I found this set and I won't rave, but I trust the Coleman name implicitly.  There was,, therefore, no doubt that I would buy this set.  For which brother, you ask?  None.  For me.  Girl Scouts don't get these, and trust me, I stuck with it, trying to earn my flatware.  I earned it.  It just took me about 40 years to collect it.  These hook together very nicely and  stay tidy when not needed.  They come apart easily but don't take that to mean they fall apart, because they don't.  When you hook them together, they stay there until you're ready to use them again and then it's a simple twist and they're separate eating tools.  This set went into my camping and bug out bag with the food, which certainly seems a logical place for it.  I have three times taken it out to show friends that Coleman really does more than lamps.  I think I'm sending new customers to Coleman and that's just a win/win for one of my favorite companies and my friends...  Thanks for another superior product, Coleman!

title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L
description:
Need a lightweight outdoor stove for backpacking, hunting, or camping? Look no further than the Coleman Portable Bottle-Top Propane Stove, which fits up to an 8-inch pan above a fully adjustable burner for precise temperature control and up to 10,000 BTUs of cooking power. Brew coffee, heat up hot dogs, and so on, knowing you can enjoy up to 2.5 hours of cooking on just one 16.4 oz. propane cylinder (sold separately). PerfectFlow technology keeps that heat steady, even in extreme conditions, and a large base offers stability for easy stirring. When its time to break down the campsite and fit all your gear into a crowded pack, youll be relieved to know that the burner and base separate from the propane cylinder for truly compact packing. Rest assured your ultra-portable stove comes with a 3-year limited warranty. The Coleman 5431A700 One-burner Propane Stove is an easy-to-use portable stove that should meet almost any camp cooking need. The PerfectFlow regulator provides consistent cooking performance by producing a steady fuel stream, even in cold weather, high altitudes, or when fuel is low. Equipped with one 10,000 BTU burner, this fully adjustable stove will last for 2.2 hours on high or up to nine hours on low. The pot supports help shield your cooking flame from the wind, while the large eight-inch burner bowl should fit most any pot. This Coleman one-burner stove can boil a quart of water in just four minutes! This stove operates on one 16.4-ounce cylinder of Coleman propane (not included). Manufacturer Warranty
Limited lifetime warranty
reviewText: I was looking for a compact burner for my disaster prep locker. Looks like I found a winner.

title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText: Quality chair slightly overpriced

It's a good cooler, its a very good cooler for $13 bucks for sure. I gave it three stars because:

It leaks horribly through the zipper. I mean it's like the top doesn't even exist. would work better if you used some of the blue ice packs from Rubbermaid rather than ice.

The shoulder strap is well I don't know. but it made me so mad adjusting it and the fact that it doesn't unclip I just cut it off.

This cooler will definitely not work well in ATV racks (I did not deduct any stars for that that is something more than I would have expected but just noting it on here.

In just 15 minutes you can start enjoying the 218 sq. ft. of shade this shelter provides. It's unique, super-stable dome shape protects from the low-angle sun in the early morning and late evening. During the day, the UVGuard material offers our best protection from the sun. The vented roof provides better ventilation, making this a great choice for large parties and family reunions.

title: Coleman Twin High Performance LED Lantern
description: Light the night your way with the Coleman high performance 580L twin LED lantern. At up to 580 lumens, this lantern lets you see far and wide while four LEDs, which never need to be replaced, provide effective, efficient, energy-saving light. The rotating dial offers customizable light settings from low to high. Long runtimes - 150 hours on low and 18 hours on high - will have you back home before you need fresh batteries. Its weather-resistant construction delivers reliable output, even in wet and windy conditions, and its base unscrews for quick access to the battery chamber so you can change them virtually anywhere, anytime.

title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Best deal for a Coleman ever.  Love it.

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText:
I was not impressed with this tent or the incredibly vague instructions.

I thought I could surely set it up by myself, but apparently not.  I struggled for quite some time.  I finally got one pole extended, and when I moved on to the next one, the fiberglass pole snapped.

I called customer service to see if they would help and they asked for a photo of the receipt.  I emailed it to them, but they said they didn't get the photo and can't help me until I get them a receipt or a picture of the date code. I checked and the photo of the receipt was definitely attached.  I will update once I get this settled.

title: Coleman High Stand,20.25" H x 18.75" W x 28" L
description: Coleman 2000020280 High Stand. Camping Furniture. Made of the highest quality materials. Camping Furniture
reviewText: Purchased this in May 2016 to hold my Coleman camp stove. When I spread it out, there was no way to "lock" mine into place as some have noted. There is nothing on the legs to lock. They just slide up but won't stay in place. So, to keep it sturdy, I purchased small hose clamps and slid them over the legs. Spread the stand apart and tighten the clamps down. Now I have a sturdy stand but I still don't trust it to hold my stove so I have a piece of plywood over the top. Didn't bother to send it back. Not worth the effort. Impulse buy.

title: Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room
description:
Watch nature unfold before your eyes in Coleman WeatherMaster 6-Person Tent with Screen Room. During the day, the screen room lets you enjoy the great outdoors away from bugs and can be used on warmer nights for extra sleeping space. If it rains, this tent keeps you dry thanks to WeatherTec technology, which uses a tub-like floor with patented corner welds, protected seams, and a covered zipper to help keep water out. Meanwhile, the strong frame is tested to withstand 35+ mph winds. Additionally, an angled window keeps rain out while allowing for extra air circulation. A hinged door makes for easy coming and going. Setup is simple with snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and a patented pin-and-ring system. Inside, there's enough room to stretch out on 2 queen-size airbeds, and storage pockets keep small items organized and off the floor. When it's time to head home, the tent easily packs up into an expandable carry bag with a rip strip for extra storage until you're off on your next adventure.


A patent-pending hinged door provides easy entry and exit, and a touch of at-home convenience.
The Coleman WeatherMaster 6 Tent has two rooms and plenty of space for the entire family. Up to six campers can sleep easily inside thanks to the 17-by-nine (W x D) footprint and 6 ft. 10 in. center height. Two doors�including a patent-pending hinged front door�offer easy in-and-out. An additional floor-less screened room provides extra ventilation on warm days, a great place for meals and gathering, rain or shine, and protected storage space. Coleman's Comfort System allows excellent ventilation control via Variflow adjustable airflow, a vented Cool-Air privacy Port, privacy vent window, and all-mesh ceiling. A convenient electrical access port lets you run a cord from your device in the tent to an outside power source, while an interior gear pocket helps you keep things organized. Setup is quick and simple thanks two color-coded poles and continuous pole sleeves, shock-corded fiberglass poles, an exclusive pin-and-ring design, and InstaClip attachments. Straightforward instructions are sewn into the convenient carry bag, and separate stake and pole bags are also included. Coleman's WeatherTec System
This proprietary series of interworking features keeps the weather where it belongs�outside the tent�at all times.
  • Leak-free Seams: In addition to fully-taped rainfly seams, the rainfly covers doors and windows, and incorporates easy-to-use Velcro frame attachments
  • Weather-resistant Fabric: Coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers are designed to keep you dry
  • Protected Seams: Inverted floor seams dramatically increase weather resistance by hiding needle holes inside the tent, away from the elements
  • Waterproof Floors: Welding technology strengthens the tent floor and eliminates needle holes
  • Zipper Protection: Zipper cuff adds protection from the elements to the door
  • Wind Strong Frame: Engineered to be a stronger, more wind-responsive frame by using redesigned poles and guy-out triangles to anchor tents and increase performance
Specifications
  • 75-denier polyester taffeta fly
  • 68-denier polyester mesh inner tent
  • 1,000-denier polyethylene floor
  • 19-millimeter steel poles
The Coleman Limited Warranty
Coleman has been in business for more than 100 years, and continues to produce high-quality, affordable tents, lanterns, table-top stoves, and more. All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and the WeatherMaster 6 Tent includes a limited one-year manufacturer's warranty. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
reviewText:
Good over all in look and quality, but smaller than I expected. The "screened-in" section is a waste of space in my mind. I mean, normally people camp right near a vehicle, so any extra coolers, belongings or gear can be stored in the vehicle (and locked). That space was too small to be sitting area or sun porch, lol! Would've been better if the bottom tarp extended the full length of the tent and it had removable sides which you could unzip if you wanted that screened-in porch thing. But in most cases it's better to have the full coverage in all three compartments.

I used double-sized air mattress and a pack-in-play on one side of the divider, than a queen-sized air mattress in the middle section. This obviously left very little space to get changed and such. Which is why I think the screen section was a waste of space.

However, for 2 adults it would be just fine as is.

The lantern runs for up to 150 hours on low and 15 hours on high using a set of eight D-cell batteries (Campmor sku # 84799). The lantern's durable housing is weather-resistant, making this lantern a solid choice for outdoor use.Weather resistant to withstand the elements.Fluorescent tube(s.Batteries: 8 D Cell(not included).Size: 7.2" x 13.6".
reviewText: Solid design with high quality materials.  I love the adjustable light output and overall brightness of the illumination when set to high.  It requires 8 D Cells, so the trade off for the long battery life and high light output is an incredibly heavy lantern.

reviewText: Nice lantern.

title: Coleman 0°F Mummy Sleeping Bag for Big and Tall Adults | North Rim Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag
reviewText: I purchased two Coleman mummy bags for my boys Scout camping trips.  I trusted the Coleman brand and these bags seemed right for the price.  They kept the boys warm and dry all year round.  During the winter months (yes, Boy Scouts camp out during the winter too) I took them off the ground and had them sleep on a cot inside their tent.  The bags worked well even in the low teens and snow.  The only complaint I had with these bags was their compressibility.  Because of their materials and thickness, they do not compress well.  Many times I found them sticking half-out of a compression sack after a campout.  But once home, they can be rolled out and properly rolled back into the sack.  My opinion, if you want an affordable, durable sleeping bag for camping, this is a good choice.

title: Coleman Bottle Top Propane Stove,Green,6.62" H x 7.81" W x 7.75" L

title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText:
Received one of these as part of a package deal with our little camper.  Honestly, only came to own one due to the package deal.  Had I set out to buy one I probably would have picked one of a more modern design that was more expensive.  I read the reviews here, including the leading review that discusses cleaning the valves.  I did that little repair (sure enough, my valves were caked with dark grease), and fired it up.  I have to say, I'm impressed with the heat this little monster puts out.  I was able to bring a small pot of water to a boil in only a few minutes.  Surprisingly quickly!

Even after cleaning the valves the flame adjustment is pretty touchy.  Only a small amount of movement at the knobs makes large changes to the flame.  Regardless, you can turn the flame down quite a bit.  The heat range seems to be from "hot" to "surface of the sun".  No using this as a food warmer, for sure.

Regardless.  Yes, is inexpensive.  Yes, there are some parts on it that are not rugged.  You'll have to be careful with the little wind screens on the sides as the little tabs that hold them on would bend easily.  The whole thing is made of fairly thin stamped metal and sounds like a hollow metal box.  Because it is.  However, I'm pretty confident that if I can avoid abusing this little stove, it should work well for a long time.

If you're like me and you need a picture for size reference, I have attached a picture of my stove with a 10" Lodge cast iron skillet and an Alpine Stowaway 1.1L pot on it.  Should be able to use both without issue.  Think two 10" skillets would be snug, but doable.

There are surely newer, fancier, hotter, more rugged stoves out there.  For what this one costs I think it'll be just fine for normal use.  Time will tell.

However, it started to leak after just one use. Not a terrible leak, just a drop every once in a while from the faucet area. It's not a big deal but still if you drive for 1-2 hours with this in your trunk, the trunk will get wet.
I went to replace it but on the way to the customer service counter with the new unit I realized that the new one is broken. that's when I decided I should probably get a different model...
Coleman is considered by many a leading brand in outdoor equipment and specifically in coolers. I was really surprised that they came up with a low quality product like this one.

title: Coleman Classic Propane Stove
description: Coleman Classic Propane Stove About Coleman More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Classic stove. All you need. The name speaks for itself.

description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. (1838.7 sq. cm) of cooking surface. It's perfect for BBQs, picnics, tailgating, camping or a night on the patio. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
reviewText:
I bought mine on a whim when I found them for 1/2 price on the Coleman website.

Pros:
Size is great for those who don't want to lug around a briefcase sized double burner style stove.
Unit comes with two generators so you can use camp fuel or kerosene.
Coleman fuel is easy to find.
BTU output is great.

Cons:
Size is not the best for backpacking.
Burn cup and pot stand fins are more flimsy than they look, they would bend up inside a backpack.
The tank body gets hot after a short while, and the flame control knob melts a bit because it is so close to the burn cup.
Pressure pump knob assembly is unique to the stove, and not as easy to come by as the standard lantern replacement part.
Kerosene not always easy to find.

Mine arrived broken right out of the box. When ignited, the flame was on full all the time, no matter where I set the valve. I called Coleman and immediately got a real person (pretty rare these days). They were stumped, but sent me a new generator free of charged. I installed the generator but got the same result. I called back again and they sent me a new needle valve (again free of charge). The valve did the trick, but took time waiting for the parts to arrive.  Also, I tried swapping the generators from Coleman fuel to Kerosene, and the exercise was a real disaster. The generator was leaking from the tank base, the fuel smelled terrible and I was unable to get the stove to light.

This stove would be best used in a canoe or kayak type of situation, where  you have more cargo storage than just your backpack.  Compared to the Coleman 533, this stove is smaller and has better flame / heat control.

In conclusion, we're all nostalgic for Coleman stoves, but I think in this instance, the micro butane variety of stoves is a better, more practical solution.

The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. reviewText: The 533 continues the long line of dependable Sportster (not 'Sporter' Amazon) stoves.  This model can use unleaded gas in addition to white gas (Coleman fuel) because of the included generator (same part as a 433 stove, but if you use a 433 generator, you'll only be able to use white gas). I prefer white gas and never got good results with 87 octane (too much soot). This stove is NOT for backpacking unless your name is Varnell, a farm boy from Georgia, over six feet tall, 230 lbs., and can tote an M60 on cross-bayou treks with no problem. Good stove to keep in the truck or in the cabin. Dependable, fires up with little problem. Easy availability of spare parts. Not as sexy as a lightweight titanium butane/propane stove, nor as weight efficient as a good commercial or homebrew alcohol stove, but gets the job done. I have made the mistake of buying and selling a number of these over the years and should have stuck with my old green model - it would still be working today.
